response.justification: Strong domain expertise in geospatial systems and proven ability to build and operate real-world geo-data platforms at scale (GISCollective, Green Map). Solid understanding of distributed systems (Dunnhumby's millions-of-requests infrastructure) and excellent engineering practices. However, the primary technical gap is severe: only 6 months of C++ experience in 2014–2015 (LabWindows/CVI, not systems programming), and no Rust. For a mid-senior individual contributor role requiring modern C++ as the primary skill, this represents significant onboarding friction. The candidate shows language-learning agility, but ramp-up time would be substantial.
strengths: Deep geospatial domain expertise with 10+ years of hands-on platform development (GISCollective, Green Map) directly applicable to routing tile systems
Proven experience operating distributed systems at scale (Dunnhumby: millions of requests/second, real-time metadata, microservices infrastructure)
Strong software engineering discipline (TDD, CI/CD, testing frameworks, paired programming) critical for data pipeline reliability
concerns: Minimal modern C++ experience (6 months at National Instruments in 2014–2015); primary skill requirement not met, and no Rust alternative listed
No explicit navigation or routing pipeline experience; geospatial work is platform/data-collection focused, not routing-tile optimization
10+ year gap since last systems-level programming work; career trajectory shows shift to higher-level languages (TypeScript, Node.js) rather than systems languages

key_factors.factor: Distributed systems and real-time data experience
